topic: NASA cancels Draper Lunar Lander project
headline: NASA DROPS DRAPER LUNAR LANDER PROJECT, TAPS BLUE ORIGIN FOR LUNAR MISSION UNDER ARTEMIS PROGRAM
summary: NASA has selected Blue Origin to construct a spacecraft to send astronauts to the moon, and has canceled the Draper Lunar Lander project. Blue Origin will design, test, and develop a lander under the Artemis program. The company is part of a national team that aims to build a lander to take astronauts back to the moon. NASA is moving forward with its lunar exploration initiative.

summary_sources.title: NASA announces that Bezos’s Blue Origin will build moon lander | Space News | Al Jazeera
Bezos floats 'national team' to build Moon lander
Spacewatch: Nasa awards first contract for lunar space station | Space | The Guardian
Chandrayaan-3 lander instruments detect 'natural' movement. Details here | Mint

sections.paragraph: NASA has selected Blue Origin to construct a spacecraft that will send astronauts to and from the surface of the moon, with a contract valued at $3.4 billion. The company will design, test, and develop a lander under the Artemis program. Meanwhile, other developments in lunar exploration include the detection of natural movement by the Chandrayaan-3 lander's instruments and the awarding of a $375 million contract to Maxar Technologies to build the power and propulsion element of the Lunar Gateway space station. Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin has also formed a 'national team' with Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and Draper to bid for the lunar landing system, aiming to send astronauts back to the moon by 2024. The goal of returning to the moon by 2024 is an ambitious one, accelerated from the original plan of 2028. Various companies and organizations are working together to make this goal a reality, with NASA's commercial and international partnerships playing a key role.
